Joyful.title: The Annunciation of the Angel to Mary
Joyful.meditation: The Angel Gabriel appears to Mary, announcing she is to be the Mother of God.

Joyful.title: The visitation of Mary to Saint Elizabeth
Joyful.meditation: Elizabeth greets Mary: “Blessed art Thou amoung women and blessed is the fruit of thy womb”

Joyful.title: The nativity of Jesus in Bethlehem
Joyful.meditation: The Virgin Mary gives birth to the Redeemer of the World. “And this will be a sign for you: you will find an infant wrapped in swaddling clothes and lying in a manger.”

Joyful.title: The presentation of Jesus to the Temple
Joyful.meditation: The Blessed Mother presents the Child Jesus in the Temple.

Joyful.title: The finding of Jesus in the Temple
Joyful.meditation: The Blessed Mother finds Jesus in the Temple.

Sorrowful.title: The Agony of Jesus in the garden
Sorrowful.meditation: At Gethsemane, Jesus prays as He contemplates the sins of the world.

Sorrowful.title: The scourging of Jesus at the pillar
The crowning with thorns
Sorrowful.meditation: Jesus is mocked and crowned with thorns.

Sorrowful.title: The Carrying of the Cross
Sorrowful.meditation: Jesus carries the heavy cross upon His shoulders to Calvary.

Sorrowful.title: The crucifixion and death of Jesus
Sorrowful.meditation: Jesus is nailed to the cross and dies after hours of agony.

Glorious.title: The Resurrection of Jesus Christ
Glorious.meditation: Jesus rises glorious and immortal, three days after His death.

Glorious.title: The Ascension of Jesus to Heaven
Glorious.meditation: Jesus ascends into Heaven forty days after His Resurrection.

Glorious.title: The descent of Holy Spirit
Glorious.meditation: The Holy Spirit descends upon Mary and the Apostles.

Glorious.title: The Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary into Heaven
Glorious.meditation: Mary is united with her Divine Son in Heaven.

Glorious.title: The Coronation of the Blessed Virgin Mary, Queen of Heaven and Earth
Glorious.meditation: Mary is gloriously crowned as Queen of Heaven and earth.

Luminous.title: The Baptism of Jesus
Luminous.meditation: The heavens open wide and the voice of the Father declares Jesus the beloved Son.

Luminous.title: The Wedding Feast at Cana
Luminous.meditation: The first of the miracles, Christ changes water into wine thanks to the intervention of Mary.

Luminous.title: The proclamation of the Kingdom of God
Luminous.meditation: Jesus proclaims the coming of the Kingdom of God.

Luminous.title: The Transfiguration
Luminous.meditation: Jesus is revealed in glory to Peter, James and John.

Luminous.title: The Institution of the Eucharist
Luminous.meditation: Christ offers his body and blood as food under the signs of bread and wine.

stations.title: Jesus Is Condemned to Death.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus, after having been scourged and crowned with thorns, was unjustly condemned by Pilate to die on the Cross.
stations.prayer: Jesus, it is because of my sins that You are going to die. Through the merits of Your sorrowful journey, help me in my journey to Heaven. I love You, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love You always and to do Your will.

stations.title: Jesus Takes Up His Cross.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus, in making this journey with the Cross on His shoulders, thought of us, and offered for us, to His Father, the death that He was about to undergo.
stations.prayer: Jesus, I embrace all the suffering that you send to me. Through the merits of your pain in carrying Your Cross, help me to carry my cross with patience and resignation. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Falls the First Time.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation:
Jesus fell for the first time under His Cross. He had been scourged and had a crown of thorns on His head, and the soldiers hit Him. He was in so

much pain He could barely walk, but He had to carry the heavy Cross.
stations.prayer: Jesus, the weight of my sins adds to your suffering and makes it infinitely worse. Through the merits of your first fall, deliver me from falling into mortal sin. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Meets His Sorrowful Mother.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus met His Mother Mary along His journey. They loved each other so deeply.
stations.prayer: Jesus, through the sorrow and joy you had in meeting your Mother Mary, help me to be truly devoted to her. Mary, help me to remember in my heart the suffering your Son underwent for me. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Simon Of Cyrene Helps Jesus Carry The Cross.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: When the Jews saw how weak Jesus was, they feared He might die on the way, before He was crucified, so they forced a man named Simon the Cyrenian to carry the Cross behind our Lord.
stations.prayer: Jesus, I accept the cross you give to me, and I accept how you want me to die. I offer all my sufferings and troubles to you. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Veronica Wipes the Face of Jesus.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: A woman named Veronica, seeing Jesus suffering so much, and sweat and blood dripping down His face, gave Him a towel to wipe His face. By a miracle, He left an image of His face on the towel.
stations.prayer: Jesus, your face was once clean and good to look upon, but blood and sweat disfigured it, and Veronica wiped Your face clean. My soul was once clean and beautiful when I was baptized, but sin disfigured it. Through your suffering, wipe my soul clean. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love You always and to do Your will.

stations.title: Jesus Falls a Second Time.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus fell the second time under the Cross. The fall renews all the pain in His body and makes His head throb.
stations.prayer: Jesus, so many times you have forgiven me, and so many times I have sinned again. Through the merits of your second fall, help me and preserve me in your grace until I die. Help me to call on you every time I am tempted. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Meets the Women of Jerusalem.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: These women wept with compassion at seeing Jesus walking to His death. But Jesus said to them, “Weep not for me but for your children.”
stations.prayer: Jesus, I am sorry for all my sins, because of the pain and sadness they cause you, who loves me so much. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Falls the Third Time.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus fell for the third time. He was so weak and the soldiers pushed Him to walk faster, but He could barely move.
stations.prayer: Jesus, by my weakness in temptation, you are going to Calvary. Give me strength to conquer temptation. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Is Stripped of His Garments.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: The soldiers snatched Jesus' robe off Him.
stations.prayer: Jesus, help me to strip my soul of bad habits so I can give all my love to you, who are so worthy of all my love. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Is Nailed to the Cross.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus was thrown down onto the Cross. He stretched out His arms and offered His life to the Father for our salvation. The soldiers nailed Him to the Cross and pushed the Cross to stand up.
stations.prayer: Jesus, keep my heart. Keep me always close to you. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love You always and to do Your will.

stations.title: Jesus Dies on the Cross.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: After suffering for three hours on the Cross, Jesus gave up His life to the Father and died.
stations.prayer: Jesus, through my sins, I deserve to be punished, but your death is my hope. Through the merits of your death, give me the grace that when I die, I will die as you want me to. I entrust my soul into your hands. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Is Taken Down from the Cross.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Two of Jesus' disciples took Jesus down from the Cross and Mary His Mother held Him close to her heart.
stations.prayer: Mary, sorrowful Mother, pray to your Son for me. Jesus, you have died because you love me. Help me to love you always. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Is Laid in the Tomb.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: The two disciples and Mary laid Jesus in the tomb.
stations.prayer: Jesus, You rose on the third day. Through your Resurrection, make me rise glorious on the last day, to be always with you in Heaven, praising and loving you. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

prayers.our_father:
Our Father, who art in heaven,
hallowed be thy name;
thy kingdom come,
thy will be done
on earth as it is in heaven.
Give us this day our daily bread,
and forgive us our trespasses,
as we forgive those who trespass against us;
and lead us not into temptation,
but deliver us from evil.

Amen.
prayers.hail_mary:
Hail, Mary, full of grace,
the Lord is with thee.
Blessed art thou among women
and blessed is the fruit of thy womb, Jesus.
Holy Mary, Mother of God,
pray for us sinners,
now and at the hour of our death.

Amen.
prayers.sign_of_cross:
In the name of the Father
and of the Son
and of the Holy Spirit. 

Amen.
prayers.apostles_creed:
I believe in God,
the Father almighty,
Creator of heaven and earth,
and in Jesus Christ, his only Son, our Lord,
who was conceived by the Holy Spirit,
born of the Virgin Mary,
suffered under Pontius Pilate,
was crucified, died and was buried;
he descended into hell;
on the third day he rose again from the dead;
he ascended into heaven,
and is seated at the right hand of God the Father almighty;
from there he will come to judge the living and the dead.

I believe in the Holy Spirit,
the holy catholic Church,
the communion of saints,
the forgiveness of sins,
the resurrection of the body,
and life everlasting.

Amen.
prayers.act_of_contrition:
Lord Jesus, Son of God,
have mercy on me, a sinner.

(from the Rite of Penance)
prayers.glory_be: Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit; as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen.
prayers.hail_holy_queen: Hail, Holy Queen, Mother of Mercy, our life, our sweetness, and our hope. To thee do we cry, poor banished children of Eve; to thee do we send up our sighs, mourning and weeping in this valley of tears. Turn then, most gracious advocate, thine eyes of mercy towards us; and after this our exile, show unto us the blessed fruit of thy womb, Jesus. O clement, O loving, O sweet Virgin Mary.
prayers.o_my_jesus: O my Jesus, forgive us our sins, save us from the fires of hell, and lead all souls to heaven, especially those in most need of thy mercy.
